  "node_id": "0017",
  "title": "The Role of Data in Publishing and Marketing",

Section text:
Game performance analytics, user behaviour tracking,
Real-time insights, A/B testing, custom dashboards
Supercell, Rovio, King, Ubisoft,
Playtika
GameSparks
Amazon Web
Services
Backend services, analytics, social features, Custom
cloud code, real-time multiplayer features
Square Enix, Bandai Namco,
Ubisoft, Konami, Sega
Kochava
Kochava
Mobile attribution, analytics, and audience
insights, Real-time data, fraud detection, audience
segmentation
Zynga, Glu Mobile, EA, King,
Ubisoft
Mixpanel
Mixpanel
Product analytics, user engagement tracking, Funnel
analysis, cohort analysis, A/B testing
Uber, Airbnb, Instacart, Slack,
DoorDash
PlayFab
Microsoft
LiveOps, player data management, monetization
tools, Real-time analytics, economy management,
matchmaking
Wizards of the Coast, Rare,
Bandai Namco, Capcom,
Square Enix
Unity Analytics
Unity Technologies
Player engagement analytics, monetization insights,
Cross-platform support, real-time data, funnel
analysis
Tencent, Zynga, EA, Ubisoft,
Square Enix
King Digital Entertainment
Makers of the famous Candy Crush Saga, King
Digital Entertainment used data analytics to pinpoint
the reason behind players dropping off the game
at level 65. The team found the gameplay element
responsible for users dropping out and eliminated it
to improve player retention rates.
Crystal Dynamics
The developers of Tomb Raider: Underworld
analysed telemetry data from over 1.5 million
players, identifying four player archetypes: runners,
solvers, pacifists, and veterans. Other key findings
included predicting game completion with 76.7%
accuracy based on early behaviour, identifying
in-game locations predictive of performance
and retention, and deciphering player archetype
evolution during gameplay.
Epic Games (Fortnite)
The popularity of Fortnite has resulted in the parent
company Epic Games processing 92 million events
a minute, with its data growing by 2 petabytes
a month. To optimize the use of this amount of
data, the company has built its analytics system
entirely on the AWS platform and uses ML tools
such as Amazon SageMaker. This enables them to
analyze player behaviour and game performance
in real time and undertake tasks such as balancing
weapons and gameplay mechanics and identifying
and fixing bugs.
26
26
Book Online
Visit Site

message.content:
**Topics:** Data analytics in game publishing and marketing, player behavior tracking, retention optimization, real-time telemetry, cloud/ML infrastructure for gaming, player segmentation, monetization tools, and LiveOps.

**Entities & Tools:** 
- Analytics Platforms: GameSparks, Kochava, Mixpanel, PlayFab, Unity Analytics
- Providers: Amazon Web Services (AWS), Microsoft, Unity Technologies
- Gaming Clients: King, Ubisoft, EA, Square Enix, Zynga, Tencent, Supercell, Rovio, Playtika, Bandai Namco, Konami, Sega, Glu Mobile, Wizards of the Coast, Rare, Capcom
- Non-Gaming Clients: Uber, Airbnb, Instacart, Slack, DoorDash
- Case Study Subjects: King Digital Entertainment (Candy Crush Saga), Crystal Dynamics (Tomb Raider: Underworld), Epic Games (Fortnite)

**Dates:** None specified in this excerpt.

**Definitions/Concepts:** LiveOps, player data management, mobile attribution, audience segmentation, funnel analysis, cohort analysis, player archetypes (runners, solvers, pacifists, veterans), telemetry data, real-time multiplayer features, custom cloud code, economy management, matchmaking, fraud detection.

**Procedures & Methods:** A/B testing, funnel and cohort analysis, real-time data tracking, telemetry collection, player archetype classification, predictive modeling, ML-driven analysis (Amazon SageMaker), cloud-native data pipeline construction, gameplay balancing, bug identification/fixing, retention optimization via targeted gameplay element removal.

**Claims & Findings:** Analytics can isolate specific gameplay mechanics causing player churn; early player behavior strongly predicts game completion; in-game location data correlates with performance and retention; player archetypes evolve dynamically during gameplay; massive-scale event data requires cloud/ML infrastructure for real-time optimization; data-driven adjustments directly improve retention rates.

**Metrics & Quantitative Data:** 1.5 million players analyzed (Tomb Raider telemetry); 76.7% accuracy in predicting game completion from early behavior; 92 million events processed per minute (Fortnite); 2 petabytes of data growth per month (Fortnite); level 65 identified as a critical drop-off point (Candy Crush).

**Evidence Types & Structure:** Structured platform comparison directory (tool, provider, feature set, client roster) followed by narrative case studies containing quantitative telemetry results, predictive modeling outcomes, and infrastructure specifications. The text implies a tabular layout for the platform directory but contains no explicit figures or rendered tables.

**Topics:** Data analytics in game publishing and marketing, player behavior tracking, retention optimization, real-time telemetry, cloud/ML infrastructure for gaming, player segmentation, monetization tools, and LiveOps.

**Entities & Tools:** 
- Analytics Platforms: GameSparks, Kochava, Mixpanel, PlayFab, Unity Analytics
- Providers: Amazon Web Services (AWS), Microsoft, Unity Technologies

- Non-Gaming Clients: Uber, Airbnb, Instacart, Slack, DoorDash
- Case Study Subjects: King Digital Entertainment (Candy Crush Saga), Crystal Dynamics (Tomb Raider: Underworld), Epic Games (Fortnite)

**Dates:** None specified in this excerpt.

**Definitions/Concepts:** LiveOps, player data management, mobile attribution, audience segmentation, funnel analysis, cohort analysis, player archetypes (runners, solvers, pacifists, veterans), telemetry data, real-time multiplayer features, custom cloud code, economy management, matchmaking, fraud detection.

**Procedures & Methods:** A/B testing, funnel and cohort analysis, real-time data tracking, telemetry collection, player archetype classification, predictive modeling, ML-driven analysis (Amazon SageMaker), cloud-native data pipeline construction, gameplay balancing, bug identification/fixing, retention optimization via targeted gameplay element removal.

**Claims & Findings:** Analytics can isolate specific gameplay mechanics causing player churn; early player behavior strongly predicts game completion; in-game location data correlates with performance and retention; player archetypes evolve dynamically during gameplay; massive-scale event data requires cloud/ML infrastructure for real-time optimization; data-driven adjustments directly improve retention rates.

**Metrics & Quantitative Data:** 1.5 million players analyzed (Tomb Raider telemetry); 76.7% accuracy in predicting game completion from early behavior; 92 million events processed per minute (Fortnite); 2 petabytes of data growth per month (Fortnite); level 65 identified as a critical drop-off point (Candy Crush).

**Evidence Types & Structure:** Structured platform comparison directory (tool, provider, feature set, client roster) followed by narrative case studies containing quantitative telemetry results, predictive modeling outcomes, and infrastructure specifications. The text implies a tabular layout for the platform directory but contains no explicit figures or rendered tables.
